sections.title: Test Coverage Analysis
sections.content:
**Bottom line:** 2 test files out of 342+ source files (~0.6% coverage). The vitest harness is wired up in `apps/api/vitest.config.ts` — it just has nothing to run.

---

sections.title: Tier 1 — Critical (security + pure logic, no mocking needed)
sections.content:
| File | Why critical |
|---|---|
| `packages/crypto/src/cipher.ts` | AES-GCM encrypt/decrypt — wrong impl silently corrupts all journal data |
| `packages/crypto/src/keys.ts` | PBKDF2 derivation, DEK wrap/unwrap, base64 helpers — key bugs are unrecoverable |
| `packages/crypto/src/recovery.ts` | Recovery codes — charset, count, hash ordering |
| `packages/core/src/habits/entry-resolution.ts` | Pure math — streak logic, completion ratio, weekly quota |
| `packages/core/src/utils/text.ts` | Word counter — HTML + JSON paths, edge cases |

sections.title: Tier 2 — High (service layer, needs repository mock)
sections.content:
| File | Biggest gap |
|---|---|
| `apps/api/src/modules/habit/habit.service.ts` | `enforceLimit` + NotFoundException + pagination delegation |
| `apps/api/src/modules/journal/journal.service.ts` | Stats aggregation, encrypted field flow |
| `apps/api/src/modules/user/user.service.ts` | `upsertBySub` idempotency, `completeOnboarding` guard |
| `apps/api/src/common/helpers/entitlement.ts` | Limit enforcement logic |
| `apps/api/src/common/pipes/zod-validation.pipe.ts` | Bad input path |

sections.title: Tier 3 — Medium (integration / controller layer)
sections.content:
Controllers, custom PWA hooks (`use-autosave`, `use-level-up`), mobile utilities.

---

sections.title: 1. `packages/core/src/habits/entry-resolution.spec.ts`
2. `packages/core/src/utils/text.spec.ts`
3. `packages/crypto/src/cipher.spec.ts`
4. `packages/crypto/src/keys.spec.ts`
5. `packages/crypto/src/recovery.spec.ts`
6. `apps/api/src/modules/habit/habit.service.spec.ts`
Edge Cases Not Covered by Existing Tests
sections.content:
| Module | Uncovered edge case |
|---|---|
| `pagination.ts` | `page > totalPages` (out-of-range page number) |
| `pagination.ts` | `limit=0` (should be clamped or handled) |
| `result.ts` | `Ok` wrapping an `Ok` (nested result) |
| `result.ts` | `Err` with a non-Error object |
| `entry-resolution.ts` | `scheduledDays: null` vs `[]` (both should count as all-days) |
| `cipher.ts` | Malformed base64 input to `decryptField` |
| `keys.ts` | Empty passphrase for `deriveMasterKey` |
| `recovery.ts` | `generateRecoveryCodes(0)` — empty array |

---

sections.title: Missing Error Handling Tests
sections.content:
- `habit.service.ts`: What happens if `Habit.create()` returns `Err(...)` — should re-throw; currently no test confirms this.
- `cipher.ts`: No test for partial/truncated encoded input (IV < 12 bytes).
- `zod-validation.pipe.ts`: No test that bad payloads produce 400 with a useful message.
- `hub-webhook.service.ts`: No test for malformed webhook payload or missing signature.

---

sections.title: Integration Test Gaps
sections.content:
The biggest integration gap is the journal creation flow that spans three layers:

```
POST /journals
  → ZodValidationPipe (validates body)
  → JournalController.create()
  → JournalService.create()
  → JournalSecurityService (encrypts fields with user DEK)
  → JournalRepository.save() (persists encrypted document)
```

No test verifies that a plaintext journal body arrives encrypted at the DB layer, which is the whole point of the feature.

All test skeletons above match the existing vitest style in `result.spec.ts` / `pagination.spec.ts` and can be dropped directly alongside those files.

codeBlocks.code:
POST /journals
  → ZodValidationPipe (validates body)
  → JournalController.create()
  → JournalService.create()
  → JournalSecurityService (encrypts fields with user DEK)
  → JournalRepository.save() (persists encrypted document)

rawOutputPreview:
Now I have everything I need. Here's the full analysis.

---

## Test Coverage Analysis

**Bottom line:** 2 test files out of 342+ source files (~0.6% coverage). The vitest harness is wired up in `apps/api/vitest.config.ts` — it just has nothing to run.

---

## Gap Priority Tiers

### Tier 1 — Critical (security + pure logic, no mocking needed)

| File | Why critical |
|---|---|
| `packages/crypto/src/cipher.ts` | AES-GCM encrypt/decrypt — wrong impl silently corrupts all journal data |
| `packages/crypto/src/keys.ts` | PBKDF2 derivation, DEK wrap/unwrap, base64 helpers — key bugs are unrecoverable |
| `packages/crypto/src/recovery.ts` | Recovery codes — charset, count, hash ordering |
| `packages/core/src/habits/entry-resolution.ts` | Pure math — streak logic, completion ratio, weekly quota |
| `packages/core/src/utils/text.ts` | Word counter — HTML + JSON paths, edge cases |

### Tier 2 — High (service layer, needs repository mock)

| File | Biggest gap |
|---|---|
| `apps/api/src/modules/habit/habit.service.ts` | `enforceLimit` + NotFoundException + pagination delegation |
| `apps/api/src/modules/journal/journal.service.ts` | Stats aggregation, encrypted field flow |
| `apps/api/src/modules/user/user.service.ts` | `upsertBySub` idempotency, `completeOnboarding` guard |
| `apps/api/src/common/helpers/entitlement.ts` | Limit enforcement logic |
| `apps/api/src/common/pipes/zod-validation.pipe.ts` | Bad input path |

### Tier 3 — Medium (integration / controller layer)

Controllers, custom PWA hooks (`use-autosave`, `use-level-up`), mobile utilities.

---

## Test Skeletons
